parameters.name: type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: There are seven types of filters:

parameters.name: effect
parameters.label: Effect
parameters.description: The extent to which the filter affects the channel (0 - not at all, 1 - maximum effect).

parameters.name: width
parameters.label: Filter Width
parameters.description: The amount of surrounding samples used in the calculation of the current sample. It is expressed in the Units.

parameters.name: widthunit
parameters.label: Filter Width Unit
parameters.description: Choose between using Samples, Frames, or Seconds as the units for this parameter.

parameters.name: spike
parameters.label: Spike Tolerance
parameters.description: For the De-spike filter type, this is the amount that a sample can differ from its neighbours without being considered a spike.

parameters.name: ramptolerance
parameters.label: Ramp Tolerance
parameters.description: When using a Ramp Preserve filter, if the input value deviates from the current output ramp value by this much, then the ramp will reset to the new input value. Otherwise the Ramp Preserve will contin

parameters.name: ramprate
parameters.label: Ramp Rate
parameters.description: When using a Ramp Preserve filter, this is the rate that the 's output channel will increase. Only if the input channel value deviates from the desired output value by 'Ramp Tolerance' amount will the

parameters.name: passes
parameters.label: Number of Passes
parameters.description: The number of times the filter is applied to the channel.

parameters.name: cutoff
parameters.label: Cutoff Frequency (Hz)
parameters.description: Decrease it if slow speed jitter is a problem.

parameters.name: speedcoeff
parameters.label: Speed Coefficient
parameters.description: Increase if high speed lag is a problem.

parameters.name: slopecutoff
parameters.label: Slope Cutoff Frequency (Hz)
parameters.description: Avoids high derivative bursts caused by jitter.

parameters.name: slopedownreset
parameters.label: Slope Down Reset
parameters.description: When On resets (bypasses) the filter effect when the downward slope exceeds the Slope Down Max value to the right.

parameters.name: slopedownmax
parameters.label: Slope Down Max
parameters.description: Set the maximum value of the slope downwards beyond which the filter resets when the toggle to the left is On.

parameters.name: slopeupreset
parameters.label: Slope Up Reset
parameters.description: When On resets (bypasses) the filter effect when the upward slope exceeds the Slope Up Max value to the right.

parameters.name: slopeupmax
parameters.label: Slope Up Max
parameters.description: Set the maximum value of the slope upwards beyond which the filter resets when the toggle to the left is On.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: When On resets (bypasses) the filter effect.

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly resets the filter effect.

parameters.name: filterpersample
parameters.label: Filter per Sample
parameters.description: Applies the filter to each sample of the channel instead of across the whole channel. Useful for working with multi-sample channels.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
